Plaintiff Edward Watson'S Motion For Summary Adjudication In Favor Of His Fifth And Sixth Causes Of Action For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Dec-01-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Munzinger of Shartsis Friese LLP telephone number 415-421-6500 for Plaintiff Edward Watson.
Nicole Dogwill and Margaret Crawford of Joseph & Cohen P.C. telephone number 415-817-9340 for Defendants Wexus Technologies, Christian Terrell, Basia Terrell and Chris Vines.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Edward Watson's motion for summary adjudication on his fifth and sixth causes of action in his first amended complaint is denied as to both causes of action. The undisputed facts establish that the actions taken by the shareholders at the November 13, 2015 shareholder meeting, in both practical and legal effect, included a vote to remove Mr. Watson as a director of Wexus and a vote to elect defendants Christian Terrell, Basia Terrell and Chris Vines as the directors of Wexus. Thus there is no basis for the declarations sought by Mr. Wexus in his fifth and sixth causes of action that the election of the individual defendants and the resolution of the new board terminating Mr. Watson as an employee and officer were invalid per California Corporations Code statutes or the governing documents of Wexus. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF EDWARD WATSON'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Sanctions is DENIED. Watson seeks to compel production of privileged documents reflecting communications between defendant Wexus Technologies, Inc. and its attorneys. Watson's moving papers relied on the repeated assertion that he was a "client." (Memo. at 1:25-27, 2:2, 7:11-12, 7:15-16, 8:7.) However, Wexus's opposition showed that it - rather than Watson or any other Wexus shareholder, officer or director - was the client and thus held the attorney-client privileges. (Id. at 2:11-22; Hoiles v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 1192, 1198.) Watson changed tack in his reply, saying he "is not claiming that he was the client," but rather "is within the privilege." (Id. at 1:21-24, 4:26.) Watson's theory is that he can pierce Wexus's attorney-client privileges now because he "would have been entitled to the requested documents had he demanded them when he was still" a Wexus director - though he did not. (Id. at 3:8-10.) Watson cites no California authority for this notion and I am aware of none. I do not find persuasive the unpublished Delaware opinion in Kirby v. Kirby. (See Rply.5:7-10.) No sanctions are awarded. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

Order To Show Cause Re: A Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRUCE B. KELSON OF SCHNADER, HARRISON & LEWIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-364-6744 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - Order To Show Cause Re: A Preliminary Injunction is Denied without prejudice. The relief requested in Plaintiff's preliminary injunction is overly broad. Furthermore, the court will not grant a preliminary injunction which allows Plaintiff to remain in the company perpetually or which enjoins the company from engaging in acts which are provided for in the company's bylaws, founding agreement, and resolutions. In light of Plaintiff's third cause of action for abuse of control, fourth cause of action for corporate waste, fifth cause of action for unjust enrichment, and seventh cause of action for violation of Business and Professions Code section 17200 et seq., the court would consider issuing an injunction which enjoins Defendant from engaging in bad acts such as the misappropriation of company resources or which grants the appointment of a receiver. The court to look at the 2 proposed orders submitted by counsel. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
